movements.notes: Controlled Articular Rotations for shoulder mobility
Dynamic warmup for posterior chain and shoulders
Prepare hinge pattern for power cleans

strength.label: Power Clean Buildup + Front Squat Volume

movements.notes: Progressive loading scheme. Focus on triple extension and fast elbows. Each set should be technically sound.
Volume work after power emphasis. Maintain upright torso and full depth.
Moderate pull on rower, sustainable pace

metcon.target_score: Complete all 5 rounds under 15 minutes. Target: 10-13 min.

movements.notes: Hip flexor, thoracic rotation, and hamstring stretch

session_why.text: This session targets power development through Olympic lifting followed by a glycolytic metcon combining the same movement pattern with pulling and monostructural work. The structure respects CNS hierarchy: power work first (fresh nervous system), then volume squatting, then conditioning. Time domain is medium (10-13 min target), emphasizing glycolytic and mixed oxidative systems without excessive interference with strength adaptation.
session_why.source: Wilson et al 2012 - Concurrent Training Meta-Analysis; Haff & Triplett - NSCA Essentials 4th ed Ch. 17
session_why.url_or_ref: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research 26(8)

movement_why.text: Power clean builds rate of force development and full-body coordination (one of Glassman's 10 physical skills). Front squat provides structural volume for quad and anterior core development. Metcon combines hinge/pull (power clean at moderate load), vertical pull (pull-ups), and monostructural cardio (rowing) to create full-body demand across multiple movement patterns. This ensures balanced development and GPP.
movement_why.source: Glassman 2002 - What is Fitness; CrossFit Level 2 Training Guide

loading_why.text: Power clean uses ramp/buildup progression (60% to 85% 1RM across 5 sets) to prepare the nervous system for heavier loads while maintaining technical quality under increasing weight. This scheme is appropriate for Week 1 or Olympic lifting sessions where technique refinement is prioritized. Front squat uses straight sets at 70% for hypertrophy stimulus and work capacity. Metcon loading at 60% 1RM allows for cycling emphasis without compromising form under fatigue.
loading_why.source: Zatsiorsky & Kraemer - Science and Practice of Strength Training Ch. 3; Bompa & Haff - Periodization 5th ed

movements.notes: Improve thoracic mobility for squat and overhead positions
Warmup squat pattern with anterior loading
Oscillating loads exploit post-activation potentiation. Heavier sets prime the nervous system, making subsequent moderate loads feel lighter. Maintain neutral spine throughout.
EMOM format maintains session flow and auto-regulates rest. Complete 3 reps at top of each 2-min window.
Release quad tension from box jumps

session_why.text: This session emphasizes strength endurance through high-volume deadlifting and pressing, followed by a 12-minute AMRAP that recycles the same patterns at lighter loads. The metcon time domain (12 min) sits at the upper end of glycolytic/mixed system work, building muscular stamina without excessive oxidative interference. Deadlift and push press create full-body demand across posterior and anterior chains.
session_why.source: Gastin 2001 - Energy System Interaction; Haff & Triplett - NSCA Essentials 4th ed Ch. 3

movement_why.text: Deadlift is the foundational hinge pattern, developing posterior chain strength (glutes, hamstrings, spinal erectors). Push press combines leg drive with vertical pressing, training the press pattern under load while adding a power component. Box jumps in the metcon develop explosive lower body power (one of the 10 physical skills). The combination ensures balanced push/pull and hinge/squat exposure.
movement_why.source: Glassman 2002 - What is Fitness; CrossFit Level 2 Training Guide

loading_why.text: Deadlift uses wave loading (75%-80%-85% repeated twice with slight increases in wave 2) to exploit post-activation potentiation. Heavy sets at 85% and 87% prime motor units, making subsequent 75%-82% sets feel lighter due to neural facilitation lasting 3-10 minutes. This scheme is appropriate for Week 2-3 strength phases. Push press uses E2MOM at 75% to build work capacity with auto-regulated rest. Metcon loads (55% deadlift, 50% push press) allow continuous cycling without technique breakdown.
loading_why.source: Robbins 2005 - Post-Activation Potentiation; Bompa & Haff - Periodization 5th ed Ch. 9
loading_why.url_or_ref: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research 19(2)

movements.notes: Controlled Articular Rotations for shoulder prep
Activate scapular retractors for pulling work
Dynamic warmup for shoulders and core
Foundation for all gymnastics. Maintain active body tension, low back pressed to floor, arms overhead. Scale to bent knees if needed.
Strict form only. Full lockout at bottom, chin over bar at top. Scale to banded pull-up if needed to maintain 5 quality reps.
Control ring stability. Full lockout at top, shoulders below elbows at bottom. Scale to box dip if needed.

movements.scaling: kipping allowed, scale to 6 reps or banded
pike push-up or box pike push-up

movements.notes: Hard effort, rest remainder of minute

metcon.target_score: Complete all work within each minute. Rest is built into the remaining time.

session_why.text: This session prioritizes gymnastics skill development and bodyweight strength. Hollow body holds build the foundational body tension required for all gymnastics movements. Strict pull-ups and ring dips develop vertical pulling and pushing strength without kipping compensation. The EMOM metcon applies these patterns under fatigue with short rest intervals, training the glycolytic system and muscular stamina. Assault bike intervals provide cardiovascular demand without additional upper body loading.
session_why.source: Sommer - Building the Gymnastic Body; Gastin 2001 - Energy System Interaction

movement_why.text: Hollow body hold is non-negotiable for gymnastics progression — it teaches active midline tension that transfers to all pulling and pressing movements. Strict pull-ups build pulling strength (vertical pull pattern) and scapular control. Ring dips develop pressing strength (vertical push) while challenging shoulder stability on unstable rings. The metcon combines these patterns with monostructural cardio to ensure balanced full-body demand and work capacity development.
movement_why.source: CrossFit Gymnastics Specialty Course; Glassman 2002 - What is Fitness

loading_why.text: All strength work uses straight sets with bodyweight loading. This is appropriate for gymnastics skill development where consistent rep quality matters more than progressive loading. Sets of 5 pull-ups and 6-8 ring dips build strength endurance in the 6-15 rep range (hypertrophy/strength-endurance zone per NSCA guidelines). EMOM metcon alternates pulling, pressing, and monostructural work with 1-minute intervals, creating 40-50 seconds of rest after each movement — adequate for glycolytic recovery but challenging for sustained output.
loading_why.source: Haff & Triplett - NSCA Essentials 4th ed Ch. 17; Bompa & Haff - Periodization 5th ed

movements.notes: T-spine mobility for overhead and catching positions
Hip flexor, hamstring, and thoracic mobility
Technical focus on explosive hip extension and fast turnover. Start each set at top of 2-min interval. Consistent load across all sets.

movements.rest: 3 min after top set, 90 sec between volume sets
movements.notes: Heavy double for neural stimulus, followed by moderate volume for structural adaptation. Maintain depth and tempo on volume sets.
Chest-to-deck, full hip extension at top

metcon.target_score: Target: 8-11 minutes. Push pace but maintain movement quality.

movements.notes: Release shoulder and lat tension from overhead work

session_why.text: This session combines Olympic lifting (hang power snatch) with heavy squatting to develop explosive power and lower body strength, followed by a glycolytic metcon emphasizing hip extension and full-body stamina. The hang power snatch builds rate of force development from the power position. Back squat provides structural strength volume. The 'For Time' metcon (8-11 min target) sits in the medium time domain, primarily glycolytic with mixed oxidative contribution.
session_why.source: Haff & Triplett - NSCA Essentials 4th ed Ch. 17; Gastin 2001 - Energy System Interaction

movement_why.text: Hang power snatch is a foundational Olympic lift that develops explosive hip extension, overhead stability, and full-body coordination (power and coordination from the 10 physical skills). Back squat is the primary lower body strength movement, targeting quads, glutes, and spinal erectors. Metcon movements (KB swings, burpees, double-unders) all emphasize hip extension and cardiovascular demand, creating full-body conditioning without additional upper body loading after overhead work.
movement_why.source: Glassman 2002 - What is Fitness; CrossFit Level 2 Training Guide

loading_why.text: Hang power snatch uses E2MOM at 70-75% 1RM with straight sets (same load across all 8 rounds). This format maintains session flow and auto-regulates rest while allowing consistent technical quality at moderate intensity. Back squat uses top set + back-off volume: a heavy double at 85% provides neural stimulus and peak intensity exposure, followed by 4x5 at 70% for hypertrophy and work capacity. This scheme efficiently combines neural and structural adaptations in one strength block.
loading_why.source: Bompa & Haff - Periodization 5th ed Ch. 9; Zatsiorsky & Kraemer - Science and Practice of Strength Training

movements.notes: Spinal mobility for hinge and press
Eccentric emphasis. Control descent, maintain neutral spine, feel stretch in hamstrings. No touch-and-go — reset each rep.
Controlled descent, explosive press. Maintain scapular retraction throughout. Consistent load across all rounds.
Wide stance, pull to chest, elbows high

session_why.text: This session targets posterior chain hypertrophy (Romanian deadlift) and horizontal pressing strength (bench press), followed by a 14-minute AMRAP that combines hinge, push, power, and monostructural elements. The RDL builds hamstring and glute strength with eccentric emphasis. Bench press develops horizontal push strength. The metcon time domain (14 min) emphasizes glycolytic and mixed oxidative systems, building work capacity across full-body movement patterns.
session_why.source: Haff & Triplett - NSCA Essentials 4th ed Ch. 17; Gastin 2001 - Energy System Interaction

movement_why.text: Romanian deadlift is the primary accessory for posterior chain development, targeting hamstrings and glutes with eccentric loading. Bench press is the foundational horizontal push, balancing the pulling volume from other sessions in a weekly program. Metcon movements ensure full-body demand: SDHP (hinge+pull), push-ups (horizontal push), box jumps (lower body power), and ski erg (monostructural cardio). This structure ensures balanced movement pattern exposure across push, pull, hinge, and power domains.
movement_why.source: Glassman 2002 - What is Fitness; CrossFit Level 2 Training Guide

loading_why.text: Romanian deadlift uses straight sets at 70% for 4x8, targeting the hypertrophy rep range (6-15 reps per NSCA guidelines). Eccentric emphasis increases time under tension for structural adaptation. Bench press uses E2MOM at 75% for 8x4, maintaining consistent volume at moderate-high intensity with auto-regulated rest. Metcon loads are moderate (95/65 lb SDHP, bodyweight push-ups and box jumps) to allow continuous cycling over 14 minutes without technique breakdown.
loading_why.source: Schoenfeld - Science and Development of Muscle Hypertrophy; Bompa & Haff - Periodization 5th ed
